delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: This course will mainly involve practicing a technique called 'mirroring'. Although originally devised to improve the suprasegmental aspects of pronunciation, it can be adapted to improve the language and body language of EFL learners when doing presentations. In this course students will progress from imitating model presenters to adopting the skills of such models in their own original presentation.
aims: This course aims to empower EFL learners to become confident and effective communicators in public settings, providing them with the necessary skills to succeed in academic, professional, and social contexts where effective presentation skills are crucial.
goals: To equip EFL learners with a range of effective presentation techniques.
To employ appropriate body language to enhance an ability to deliver impactful presentations.
To structure content, using visual aids.

plan.text: Course Overview: Introduction to different forms of public communication
What is 'mirroring'? Examine an example from Meyers' workshop
Transcription of short segment of mirroring model
Lecture on segmental and suprasegmental features for later identification in model.
Analysis of transcription: Highlight segmental and suprasegmental features of mode.
Consultations with the teacher to find more suprasegmentals
Class presentations of Mirroring project Number 1
Choose model for second mirroring project
Transcription of short segment of mirroring model
Analysis of transcription: Highlight segmental and suprasegmental features of mode.
Consultations with the teacher to find more suprasegmentals
Class presentations of Mirroring project Number 2

textbooks: The teacher will provide all materials
prereq: Students should be clearly willing to communicate and to do class presentations
prep: Students may need to spend significant time outside of class preparing for class activities.
teachers: ◎ ショーン バーゴイン
